stories.title: Full 2026 All-Star Rosters Out — Harper Gets Legend Pick, 26 First-Timers
stories.angle: Full 32-player AL and NL rosters unveiled for the 96th MLB All-Star Game (July 14, Citizens Bank Park). Three clubs tied for most selections at 5: Braves, Dodgers, Phillies. Commissioner Rob Manfred invoked a special "Legend Pick" for Bryce Harper — nine-time All-Star, playing in his home park. Shea Langeliers (Athletics) starts at catcher — first A's catcher to start the ASG since Terry Steinbach in 1988. Nick Kurtz starts at 1B for the AL. Freddie Freeman earns 10th selection; Chris Sale earns his 10th as well. 26 first-timers total across both rosters.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The 2026 MLB All-Star rosters are out.

Bryce Harper was added via Commissioner's "Legend Pick" — playing in his home park at Citizens Bank Park. Nine-time All-Star. "I deserve to be in the game."

Hard to argue.

#BallparkBanter #AllStarGame #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
26 first-timers. Dodgers, Braves, Phillies each send 5.

Shea Langeliers starts at catcher for the AL — first A's catcher to start the All-Star Game since Terry Steinbach in 1988.

Freeman and Sale both earn their 10th All-Star selections.

#BallparkBanter #AllStarGame #MLB

stories.title: The 2026 All-Star Snubs — Turang, Murakami, Gray, Wheeler Deserved Better
stories.angle: The best omissions from the 2026 All-Star rosters. Brice Turang (Brewers 2B) ranks No. 7 in NL bWAR — the biggest statistical snub of the year. Munetaka Murakami (White Sox) has 20 HR in only 57 games and was left off. Sonny Gray (Red Sox) is 9-1 with a 2.69 ERA but missed the AL cut. Zack Wheeler (Phillies) and Willson Contreras (Cardinals) round out the case for the All-Snub Team.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
2026 All-Star Snub Team:

Turang — No. 7 NL WAR. Left out.
Murakami — 20 HR in 57 games. Left out.
Gray — 9-1, 2.69 ERA. Left out.
Wheeler — solid numbers. Left out.
Contreras — best positional snub. Left out.

#BallparkBanter #MLB #AllStarGame

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Munetaka Murakami has 20 HR in 57 games and did not make the All-Star Game.

For context: that is historically elite home run pace. The White Sox are 47-42.

The AL Central is baseball's best story this year. Where's the representation?

#BallparkBanter #WhiteSox #MLB

stories.title: Home Run Derby Field Update — Schwarber Now In, Judge Out, Netflix July 13
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber (30 HR, MLB leader) gave two conditions for participating: healthy and on the All-Star team. He just made the All-Star team. Health is now the only barrier. Caminero (Rays, 2025 runner-up) was first confirmed. Judge (rib fracture, IL) is out. Ben Rice (Yankees, 22 HR) is interested. Alvarez opted out. Derby streams live on Netflix July 13, 8 PM ET. New swing-based format: 20 swings Round 1, 15 swings Rounds 2 and 3.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber's two conditions for the Home Run Derby:
1. Be healthy
2. Make the All-Star Game

He just made the All-Star Game.

MLB HR leader with 30. If he's healthy — he's in.

Caminero confirmed. Judge out. Netflix. July 13.

#BallparkBanter #HomeRunDerby #MLB #Phillies

stories.title: Skubal Trade — 28 Days to Deadline, Braves Still Frontrunners
stories.angle: August 3 trade deadline is 28 days away. Tarik Skubal remains the most coveted chip — 85% trade probability per ESPN's Passan and McDaniel. Braves frontrunner (Nightengale) with Strider on the IL and Elder struggling. Contract: $32M. Detroit went 38-49 through July 5 and remains a seller. Other interested parties: Brewers, Cubs, Phillies, Padres, Rays, Red Sox.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal trade watch: 28 days to the August 3 deadline.

ESPN puts his trade probability at 85%. The Braves are the frontrunner with Spencer Strider on the IL.

The Tigers are 38-49. They're selling. It's coming.

#BallparkBanter #MLB #TradeDeadline #Tigers #Braves

stories.title: Milestone Watch — Ramírez Nears 300/300 Club, Sanchez Breaks 115-Year Phillies Record
stories.angle: Two milestone stories worth knowing heading into All-Star Weekend. (1) José Ramírez hit his 300th career stolen base and now needs 9 more home runs to join the ultra-exclusive 300 HR / 300 SB club. He also became the first Guardians player to homer against all 29 other MLB teams. (2) Cristopher Sanchez (Phillies) extended his scoreless inning streak to 41⅔ — breaking a 115-year franchise record held by Grover Cleveland Alexander. His first All-Star selection is his 2026 reward.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Two milestones worth watching:

Ramírez hit his 300th career SB. Needs 9 HR to join the 300/300 club — only 4 players ever.

Cristopher Sanchez: 41⅔ scoreless innings. Broke a 115-year Phillies record set by Grover Cleveland Alexander.

#BallparkBanter #MLB
